text:
Deep in the heart of Lapland, there lies a cozy cottage tucked amongst dense fir branches. Every winter, the village gathers here for the Midnight Snow Ball. It's a tradition so old that nobody remembers how it started. They talk about it in hushed voices for days before it happens.

This year's hostess, Olga, has baked the biggest pile of lemming cake anyone has ever seen. Tonight will be a festival of fire lights. As the last ember flickers into a serene glow, a mischievous squirrel crashes through the window. Its eyes sparkle like stars.

"I've stolen some cheese from Mrs. Snowy's henhouse!" it squeaks, dancing around the ember.

The villagers look on with anticipation. "Bring it here!"

It scuttles out, cheese clutched between its teeth. Olga smiles. "Remember the old song 'Ye mice of Lapland?' You'll have to hum it."
